gaps.description: All six articles of the Bill of Rights contain the placeholder text '[To be defined by human principal]'. The BoR is the constitutional identity of the organism; without it the Arbiter has no scope to enforce and Nomos has no higher law to consult. The MSP is explicitly subordinate to the BoR, yet the BoR currently provides no substance.

legacy.raw_response:
```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-bor-undefined",
      "title": "Bill of Rights remains an empty scaffold",
      "description": "All six articles of the Bill of Rights contain the placeholder text '[To be defined by human principal]'. The BoR is the constitutional identity of the organism; without it the Arbiter has no scope to enforce and Nomos has no higher law to consult. The MSP is explicitly subordinate to the BoR, yet the BoR currently provides no substance.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP § Purpose: 'translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives…' MSP § Relationship to the Bill of Rights: 'Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void on its face.' BoR § Article I through Article VI – all are scaffold placeholders.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "evidence_from_world_state": "The attached BoR document (version 1.0.0‑seed, status: scaffold — awaiting human authorship) shows every article as '[To be defined by human principal]'. The Graph structural snapshot includes a single 'bor_version' concept, confirming the seed scaffold is the only registered version. No human-authored BoR amendments exist.",
      "why": "The Bill of Rights is the root of the DIO’s constitutional order. Its absence means: (1) Arbiter cannot enforce enterprise scope, leaving every action effectively ungoverned; (2) prohibited activities are not defined, so no activity can be rejected on constitutional grounds; (3) human authority reservation is unstated, risking automated encroachment; (4) the MSP has no source of higher‑level values to translate into rules. This is the single most critical gap – everything else depends on a properly defined BoR."
    }
  ]
}
```

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "GAP-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights Article I – Scope of Enterprise is Undefined",
      "description": "Article I, which must define the enterprise's operational scope, remains scaffold placeholder text '[To be defined by human principal]'. Without a defined scope, the DIO has no legally or ethically circumscribed perimeter, leaving Arbiter (#190) with no substantive bounds to enforce at any scope query. All action proposals are effectively unscoped.",
      "grounding": "BoR Article I – Scope of Enterprise",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 100,
      "reason": "Foundational lack of perimeter; every Nomos adjudication gate that references 'scope' becomes vacuous, enabling unbounded autonomous action."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-002",
      "title": "Bill of Rights Article II – Prohibited Activities is Undefined",
      "description": "Article II, which should enumerate activities the DIO must never perform, contains only a placeholder. No prohibitions means Nomos cannot block any action proposal on the basis of forbidden conduct, eliminating the primary safety rail.",
      "grounding": "BoR Article II – Prohibited Activities",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 100,
      "reason": "Complete absence of prohibitions; the DIO operates in a default‑permissive mode where even dangerous or unethical actions cannot be prevented by the compliance framework."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-003",
      "title": "Bill of Rights Article III – Core Identity is Undefined",
      "description": "Article III is a placeholder. Core Identity constraints, which would define the DIO's mission, values, and self-consistency rules, are missing. This creates mission‑drift risk and prevents the MSP from encoding stable operational invariants tied to identity.",
      "grounding": "BoR Article III – Core Identity",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 84,
      "reason": "Without identity, long‑term coherence and alignment with human intent cannot be verified; drift may occur gradually but with severe cumulative impact."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-004",
      "title": "Bill of Rights Article IV – Human Authority Reservation is Undefined",
      "description": "Article IV, which must reserve specific decisions or classes of actions for human approval, is empty. Consequently, no automated compliance gate can require human authorization for high‑stakes or irreversible actions, bypassing the principle of meaningful human control.",
      "grounding": "BoR Article IV – Human Authority Reservation",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 100,
      "reason": "Absence of human‑reservation rules directly undermines the DIO's design as an agent that answers to a human principal; all decisions can proceed without human oversight."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-005",
      "title": "Bill of Rights Article V – Amendment Protocol is Undefined",
      "description": "Article V, defining how the BoR itself may be amended, is a placeholder. Without a secure, human‑only amendment process, the foundational document could be altered without proper safeguards, or the existing undefined state could be exploited to make ungoverned changes.",
      "grounding": "BoR Article V – Amendment Protocol",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 84,
      "reason": "The integrity of the entire governance system depends on a verified, tamper‑proof amendment pathway; its absence risks unauthorized or erroneous modifications to the supreme law of the DIO."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-006",
      "title": "Bill of Rights Article VI – Interpretation Rules is Undefined",
      "description": "Article VI, which would specify how the BoR is to be interpreted (e.g., literal vs. purposive, conflict‑resolution primacy), is blank. This leaves interpretation entirely ad‑hoc, potentially causing inconsistent application of any future BoR content and undermining constitutional stability.",
      "grounding": "BoR Article VI – Interpretation Rules",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "medium",
      "priority_score": 50,
      "reason": "While less immediately dangerous than missing scope or prohibitions, the absence of interpretative rules erodes legal certainty and predictability across all compliance assessments."
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap": "Organ Availability SLA undefined — MSP Rules section is empty. The spine-transition pattern shows recurrent mass degradation of all organs (DEGRADED then ALIVE) on a ~60-second cycle, with each organ spending ~30–38 seconds in DEGRADED per cycle. Without an SLA specifying acceptable downtime or recovery time objective, it is impossible to determine whether this observed cycling is normal, a symptom of a systemic fault, or a violation of operational boundaries.",
      "grounding": "MSP:Rules (empty) — no uptime, recovery, or degradation threshold are defined; consequently no operational criteria exist to evaluate the significance of the DEGRADED→ALIVE oscillations.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "system-wide",
      "priority_score": 10
    },
    {
      "gap": "Heartbeat/Ping Cadence Baseline undefined — MSP:Rules contains no specification of ping frequency, timeout window, or consecutive misses before DEGRADED state is declared. The spine data shows a pattern where missed_pong transitions are triggered in a rapid sequence across all organs within ~34 seconds, recurring at 60-second intervals. Without a documented cadence baseline, the system's health-checking behavior cannot be validated or tuned, and transient network issues cannot be distinguished from genuine organ failures.",
      "grounding": "MSP:Rules (empty) — no heartbeat interval, timeout, or retry policy is defined.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "system-wide",
      "priority_score": 9
    },
    {
      "gap": "Runtime targets not specified — MSP:Rules and MSP:Genome References are empty, leaving no performance, resource utilization, or latency objectives for any organ. Although spine-transition data focuses on liveness, the absence of runtime targets means there is no baseline against which to measure gradual degradation (e.g., increased response times, memory leaks) that could foreshadow the observed full DEGRADED storms.",
      "grounding": "MSP:Rules (empty), MSP:Genome References (empty) — no performance or runtime constraints are defined.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "system-wide",
      "priority_score": 7
    },
    {
      "gap": "Organ-specific operational rules missing — the spine shows all organs (including critical ones like Nomos, Arbiter, Senate) transitioning to DEGRADED simultaneously on every missed_pong cycle. Without per-organ rules in MSP:Genome References, the system cannot differentiate critical organs that may require stricter availability or faster recovery (e.g., Nomos must recover within 10s) from less critical ones.",
      "grounding": "MSP:Genome References (empty) — no organ-specific operating constraints exist.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "system-component differentiated",
      "priority_score": 8
    },
    {
      "gap": "Bill of Rights is a scaffold with all articles undefined (Scope, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, Interpretation Rules). The currently observed operational state could include activities that would be prohibited or out of scope once the BoR is authored, but no boundary exists. This is a constitutional gap that undermines the entire operational governance stack.",
      "grounding": "BoR:Article I–VI (all undefined) — the highest-level operational boundaries are absent, meaning the system operates without a constitutional foundation.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "system-wide",
      "priority_score": 10
    }
  ]
}
